## Automated Tests Required

### LS-004 — Concurrency control: forward-drop, respawn-queue, abort recovery — Logic
**Test file path**: `tests/unit/level_streaming/level_streaming_concurrency_test.gd`
**What to test**:
- AC-1: Forward `transition_to_section` while `_transitioning == true` is DROPPED with `push_warning` containing target_id
- AC-2: `reload_current_section(save_game)` while `_transitioning == true` sets `_pending_respawn_save_game = save_game`; in-flight transition completes; step 13 fires the queued respawn with `reason = RESPAWN`
- AC-3: Last-wins on second queue-while-queued — `_pending_respawn_save_game = save_B` after second call
- AC-4: `_abort_transition()` resets all state — `_transitioning = false`, LOADING popped, fade alpha 0.0, all pending fields cleared, state IDLE
- AC-5: Step 13 drain clears `_pending_respawn_save_game` BEFORE re-entrant call
- AC-6: Full forward → respawn-queued → step-13-drain integration scenario (`current_section_id == stub_b` at re-entry, not plaza)
- AC-7: `reload_current_section` is a thin facade — same coroutine path as `transition_to_section(_, _, RESPAWN)`
- AC-8: Worst-case end-to-end ≤1.14s; CI threshold 1.5s with warning, min-spec deferred to LS-010

**Edge cases to cover**:
- Forward → forward dropped → real respawn queued (AC-1+AC-2 chain)
- Queue passed null as second call (effectively cancels — last-wins semantics)
- `_abort_transition` called from IDLE state (defensive — no-op for state, still resets pending queues)
- `_current_section_id == &""` then `reload_current_section` (registry-miss path at step 4)

**Estimated test count**: ~12 unit tests (8 ACs + 4 edge-case variants)

---

### LS-005 — Registry failure paths + ErrorFallback CanvasLayer recovery — Integration
**Test file path**: `tests/integration/level_streaming/level_streaming_failure_recovery_test.gd`
**What to test**:
- AC-1: `_simulate_registry_failure()` flips `_registry_valid = false`; subsequent `transition_to_section` immediately `push_error`s and returns; `_transitioning` remains false
- AC-2: Registry-miss at step 4 — `_abort_transition` runs BEFORE `queue_free`; outgoing scene still in tree (`is_instance_valid` returns true at abort time)
- AC-3: ResourceLoader.load null → `_abort_transition` + `change_scene_to_file("res://scenes/ErrorFallback.tscn")` invoked
- AC-4: PackedScene.instantiate null → same recovery path as AC-3
- AC-5: add_child failure → same recovery path (defensive; rare)
- AC-6: ErrorFallback `_ready` displays `_last_error_message`; auto-routes to MainMenu after 2s in debug
- AC-7: Single recovery function — code-review check that every failure branch ends with `_abort_transition() + push_error/show_error_fallback + return`
- AC-9: Clean-state invariants after every failure path: `_transitioning == false`, `_state == IDLE`, LOADING NOT on stack, `_pending_respawn_save_game == null`, fade alpha 0.0

**Edge cases to cover**:
- Registry entry deliberately points at non-existent file `res://scenes/sections/__bad__.tscn`
- Test fixture: deliberately-malformed `.tscn` for instantiate-null path
- ErrorFallback.tscn missing → defense-in-depth direct-route to MainMenu
- Boot-time `_registry_valid = false` (autoload bring-up failure)

**Estimated test count**: ~10 integration tests (8 ACs minus AC-6/AC-8 manual-evidence deferrals + edge cases)

---

### LS-006 — Same-section guard + focus-loss + cache mode + memory invariant — Logic + Integration
**Test file paths**:
- `tests/unit/level_streaming/level_streaming_guard_cache_test.gd` (AC-1..AC-7)
- `tests/integration/level_streaming/level_streaming_focus_memory_test.gd` (AC-8, AC-9)

**What to test**:
- AC-1: Same-section forward in shipping returns early — no state change, no LOADING push, no signals
- AC-2: Same-section forward in debug fires `assert(false, ...)` — captured via test infrastructure
- AC-3: Same-section RESPAWN proceeds normally — guard does NOT block respawns; `section_exited` + `section_entered` both fire with reason RESPAWN
- AC-4: `CACHE_MODE_REUSE` used at step 5 (lint check on source file)
- AC-5: Cached second-load is faster — `ResourceLoader.has_cached(path) == true` after first; second SWAPPING duration ≤50% of first (CI tolerance widened to 70%)
- AC-6: `evict_section_from_cache(section_id)` exists as no-op stub
- AC-7: `project.godot` `[application]` section contains `run/pause_on_focus_lost=true`
- AC-8: NOTIFICATION_APPLICATION_FOCUS_IN with `_state == FADING_OUT` snaps `_fade_rect.color.a = 1.0`; coroutine resumes; final state IDLE
- AC-9: `OS.get_static_memory_usage()` peak after second `plaza` ≤110% of peak after first `plaza` (round-trip plaza → stub_b → plaza)

**Edge cases to cover**:
- Shipping-build emulation via `_simulate_shipping_build` helper
- gdunit4 `assert` capture pattern (may need wrapping helper if `assert` cannot be intercepted directly)
- Focus regain during IDLE (no-op snap; alpha stays 0.0)
- SectionBoundsHint missing → fallback `_derive_aabb_from_children`

**Estimated test count**: ~14 tests (~10 unit + ~4 integration)

---

### LS-007 — F5/F9 quicksave/quickload queue during transition — Integration
**Test file path**: `tests/integration/level_streaming/level_streaming_quicksave_queue_test.gd`
**What to test**:
- AC-1: `_pending_quicksave: bool = false` and `_pending_quickload_slot: int = -1` initialized post-boot
- AC-2: `queue_quicksave_or_fire()` in IDLE fires `SaveLoad.save_to_slot(0, ...)` directly; `Events.game_saved` fires with slot=0
- AC-3: `queue_quickload_or_fire()` in IDLE with slot 0 occupied fires `SaveLoad.load_from_slot(0)` and launches LOAD_FROM_SAVE transition
- AC-4: F5 during FADING_IN sets `_pending_quicksave = true`; on FADING_IN → IDLE, `Events.game_saved` fires once
- AC-5: Both F5+F9 queued during transition — quicksave fires first on drain, quickload second; ordering verified
- AC-6: Idempotent re-queuing — second F5 within window does not double-fire; only one save on drain
- AC-7: `_abort_transition` clears `_pending_quicksave = false` AND `_pending_quickload_slot = -1`
- AC-9: Save/Load Story 007 handler delegates to `LSS.queue_quicksave_or_fire()` (NOT `SaveLoad.save_to_slot(0)` directly) — code-review check

**Edge cases to cover**:
- F5+F9+respawn-queue all queued — documented as best-effort consistent
- `_assemble_quicksave_payload` returns null → return without firing; no `Events.game_saved` emit
- Slot 0 empty for quickload → `Events.hud_toast_requested(&"quicksave_unavailable", {})` instead of load
- Second F5 while pending — no change to `_pending_quicksave` (idempotent)

**Estimated test count**: ~11 integration tests

---

### LS-008 — Section authoring contract + stub scenes + Environment — Config/Data
**Test file paths**:
- `tests/unit/level_streaming/section_authoring_contract_test.gd` (content-introspection of stub scenes — AC-1, AC-2, AC-6, AC-7, AC-8, AC-9)
- `tests/integration/level_streaming/section_environment_assignment_test.gd` (runtime — AC-3, AC-4, AC-5)

**What to test**:
- AC-1: `res://scenes/sections/plaza.tscn` exists with: Node3D root in group `section_root`, exports `section_id == &"plaza"`, distinct EntryPoint + RespawnPoint Marker3D, SectionBoundsHint MeshInstance3D (BoxMesh 30×10×30), Floor StaticBody3D with surface_tag meta `&"default"`
- AC-2: `res://scenes/sections/stub_b.tscn` mirrors structure with `section_id == &"stub_b"`; ~30 nodes total
- AC-3: CR-9 assertion in debug — broken fixture (no `section_root` group) triggers `push_error` with rule name; transition continues (debug-only fence)
- AC-4: Section's non-null Environment resource is assigned to `get_viewport().get_camera_3d().get_world_3d().environment` after transition
- AC-5: Section's null Environment falls back to `res://assets/data/default_environment.tres`
- AC-6: 5-rule smoke check parameterized over both scenes: (a) Node3D root, (b) section_root group, (c) section_id matches, (d) entry/respawn distinct Marker3D, (e) non-zero section_bounds AABB
- AC-7: `_compute_section_bounds()` derives AABB from `SectionBoundsHint.get_aabb()` × `global_transform`; non-zero AABB
- AC-8: Floor StaticBody3D `has_meta("surface_tag") == true` AND `get_meta("surface_tag") == &"default"`
- AC-9: Both stub scenes use `class_name SectionRoot` script (single-script pattern)

**Edge cases to cover**:
- Missing Camera3D in new scene — Environment assignment test skips
- SectionBoundsHint missing — fallback `_derive_aabb_from_children`
- `default_environment.tres` missing — log warning; world environment may stay at previous
- Future section scenes added to registry — must satisfy contract

**Estimated test count**: ~14 tests (~10 unit + ~4 integration)

### LS-009 — Anti-pattern fences + lint guards + CR-13 sync-subscriber — Config/Data
**Test file paths**:
- `tests/unit/level_streaming/level_streaming_lint_test.gd` (AC-1..AC-4, AC-6, AC-8)
- `tests/unit/level_streaming/level_streaming_sync_subscriber_test.gd` (AC-5, AC-7)

**What to test**:
- AC-1: Registry entry `unauthorized_reload_current_section_caller` — severity HIGH, owning_adr `adr-0007`, allowlist (Mission Scripting, F&R, Menu System, LSS itself)
- AC-2: Registry entry `cross_section_nodepath_reference` — severity HIGH; smoke check parses NodePaths at scene-load time
- AC-3: Registry entry `missing_register_restore_callback` — severity MEDIUM; runtime warn at first transition if `_restore_callbacks.is_empty()`
- AC-4: Registry entry `bypass_thirteen_step_protocol` — severity HIGH; grep on `change_scene_to_(file|packed)`; allowed only in LSS + main.gd
- AC-5: Registry entry `section_exited_subscriber_awaits` — severity MEDIUM; runtime check via `Engine.get_process_frames()` pre/post `Events.section_exited.emit`
- AC-6: Lint test functions present for AC-1..AC-4 (4 grep patterns); CR-13 separate runtime test
- AC-7: Async subscriber to `section_exited` (does `await get_tree().process_frame`) → `push_error` with "CR-13 violation" in debug build
- AC-8: Lint failure messages identify pattern name + file + line + matched text + ADR/CR citation
- AC-9: All 5 pattern names cross-referenced in `docs/architecture/control-manifest.md` Foundation layer section

**Edge cases to cover**:
- Test fixture (deliberate violation) at `tests/fixtures/level_streaming/violation_unauthorized.gd` excluded from production lint scope
- Registry file extension (already populated by save-load Story 009 + localization Story 005 — this story extends, not creates)
- Shipping build → CR-13 check skipped (debug-only fence)

**Estimated test count**: ~9 tests (5 registry checks + 4 grep + sync-subscriber runtime)

---

### LS-010 — Performance budget P90 measurement + VERBOSE_TRANSITION_LOGGING — Logic
**Test file path**: `tests/integration/level_streaming/level_streaming_perf_p90_test.gd`
**What to test**:
- AC-1: `VERBOSE_TRANSITION_LOGGING_ENABLED` flag gates step-level instrumentation; `_step_timings` Dictionary populated when enabled, untouched when disabled
- AC-2: Step-level timestamps captured for steps 1, 2, 3, 5, 6, 7, 9, 10, 12, 13 — each value positive int µs
- AC-3: Harness runs 10 transitions plaza → stub_b → plaza alternating; captures durations from step 1 entry to FADING_IN → IDLE
- AC-4: p90 ≤ 570000µs (570ms) AND max ≤ 800000µs (800ms); on individual run failure, repeat 10-run set once; FAIL only if both sets contain failing run
- AC-5: SWAPPING phase isolation (step 3 → step 10) ≤ 500ms across all runs
- AC-6: Memory invariant cross-reference to Story 006's `level_streaming_focus_memory_test.gd` — peak ≤110% second-transition / first
- AC-7: Perf report doc at `production/qa/evidence/level_streaming_perf_p90_[date].md` written with: hardware, Godot version, 10-run table, stats, slowest-run breakdown, PASS/FAIL verdict
- AC-8: CI vs min-spec verdict differentiation — CI threshold 800ms p90 (50% headroom over min-spec); failures on CI but PASS on min-spec downgraded to advisory FAIL
- AC-9: Regression detection — adding a hypothetical 200ms step-9 callback would push p90 over budget AND identify step 9 as dominant in slowest-run breakdown

**Edge cases to cover**:
- CI runner detection via `CI=true` env var (GitHub Actions standard)
- Aborted transition → only steps before abort recorded (acceptable diagnostic state)
- ResourceLoader cold-cache first run typically slowest; cached subsequent runs faster
- Filesystem write failure for report doc → log warning, test still asserts pass/fail from in-memory data
- Iris Xe min-spec verification DEFERRED per TD-002 — manual evidence stub written

**Estimated test count**: ~6 integration tests (perf is fewer functions but each runs 10 sub-iterations)

---

### PIC-FIX — TD-009: `player_interact_cap_warning.gd` resolver bug — Logic
**Test file path**: `tests/unit/core/player_character/player_interact_cap_warning_test.gd` (existing — fix the 5 failing assertions)
**What to test (already specified by failing tests)**:
- `test_resolve_cap_exceeded_returns_within_cap` (currently 2 assertion failures) → cap-exceeded path must return the within-cap target, not null
- `test_resolve_cap_one_returns_a_stub` (currently 2 assertion failures) → cap=1 path must return the stub, not null
- `test_resolve_within_cap_returns_priority_winner` → priority resolver must pick Document(0) over Door(3)/Pickup(2) — lower priority value wins

**Edge cases to cover**:
- Sprint 06 sign-off suspected PhysicsServer3D space pollution (test isolation) — Sprint 07 re-investigation suggests resolver itself is dominant root cause; verify which is actually broken before patching
- Confirm cap-edge null-return is removed (return within-cap target instead)
- Priority-value semantic: lower number = higher priority (per existing Document(0) / Pickup(2) / Door(3))

**Estimated test count**: 0 new tests; 5 assertion fixes (turning RED → GREEN)

## Manual QA Checklist

### LS-005 — ErrorFallback shipping-build behavior — Integration (AC-6 / AC-8)
**Verification method**: Manual export-build inspection + screenshot
**Who must sign off**: lead-programmer
**Evidence to capture**: Screenshot of ErrorFallback flash (or skipped state) in shipping build; screenshot of debug-build 2-second-then-MainMenu auto-route
**Evidence file**: `production/qa/evidence/level_streaming_shipping_error_fallback.md`

Checklist:
- [ ] Debug build: triggering a registry-miss shows ErrorFallback.tscn for ≥2s with the error message visible, then auto-routes to MainMenu
- [ ] Shipping build: triggering a registry-miss either flashes briefly or routes directly to MainMenu (per CR-3 — implementation choice documented)
- [ ] In both builds: no crash, no orphan scene state, player ends up at MainMenu

**Note**: Shipping-build verification deferred until export pipeline is stable; can be filed as advisory evidence stub if blocked.

---

### LS-007 — F5 mid-transition produces post-transition save chime — Integration (AC-8)
**Verification method**: Manual playtest with audio monitoring
**Who must sign off**: qa-tester
**Evidence to capture**: Video clip OR written notes confirming chime fires AFTER snap-reveal completes (not during the cut); save card visible when Menu opened post-transition
**Evidence file**: `production/qa/evidence/level_streaming_f5_during_transition.md`

Checklist:
- [ ] Trigger a section transition (e.g., walk into a transition trigger volume)
- [ ] During the FADING_OUT/SWAPPING phase, press F5
- [ ] Observe: NO save-confirm chime during the transition cut
- [ ] After snap-reveal completes (player regains control), save-confirm chime audible
- [ ] Open Menu → slot 0 has a fresh save with current section captured

**Note**: Full Audio integration depends on Audio epic (already shipped Sprint 07 — chime hookup should be in place).

**Who must sign off**: technical-artist + lead-programmer

- [ ] p90 ≤ 570ms; max ≤ 800ms
- [ ] Per-step breakdown for slowest run captured
- [ ] If FAIL: regression triage identifies offending step; ADR-0008 G2 verification flag updated

**Note**: This evidence is DEFERRED per ADR-0008 G2 + TD-002. Sprint 08's deliverable is the harness + dev-hardware measurement; Iris Xe verification is a future hardware-availability task.

## Smoke Test Scope

Critical paths to verify before QA hand-off for Sprint 08:

1. **Game launches to MainMenu without crash**
2. **NEW_GAME path** — main menu → MainMenu starts a new game → first section loads via LSS (plaza.tscn)
3. **FORWARD transition** — trigger transition_to_section to stub_b → fade-out, swap, fade-in completes ≤0.57s p90
4. **RESPAWN transition** — F&R-driven respawn-in-place reloads current section with saved state
5. **Concurrency** — rapid F&R + section-trigger sequence does not deadlock; in-flight transitions drop forward calls and queue respawns
6. **Failure recovery** — registry-miss / load-fail / instantiate-fail all route to ErrorFallback then MainMenu without orphaned state
7. **Quicksave/Quickload during transition** — F5 mid-transition does not lose intent; save fires post-transition with chime
8. **Save/load round-trip across section transition** — quicksave in plaza → quickload → re-enters plaza with state intact
9. **Performance budget** — 10-run harness PASSES on dev hardware; CI threshold 800ms p90; min-spec deferred per TD-002
10. **Memory invariant** — round-trip plaza → stub_b → plaza shows ≤110% peak heap (no leak)
11. **Anti-pattern fences** — lint tests pass; no unauthorized callers of `transition_to_section` / `reload_current_section`; no async `section_exited` subscribers
12. **Existing regression baseline** — Sprint 04..07 outputs (stealth AI, mission/F&R, HUD, audio, document collection, post-process stack) all green; cumulative test suite has ZERO new failures (TD-009's 5 failures CLOSED via PIC-FIX)

*Smoke tests are verified via `/smoke-check sprint`. Reference this list when running that skill.*

---

## Playtest Requirements

| Story | Playtest Goal | Min Sessions | Target Player Type |
|-------|---------------|--------------|--------------------|
| LS-007 | Confirm F5 mid-transition chime feels natural (not jarring) | 1 | Anyone (subjective check) |
| LS-008 | Confirm `pause_on_focus_lost` UX feel during alt-tab + window-switch (CR-15 manual; no unit test for "feel") | 1 | Anyone |
| LS-010 | Min-spec Iris Xe verification (DEFERRED — hardware availability) | 1 | Tech artist on min-spec |

**Sign-off requirement**: Playtest notes written to `production/session-logs/playtest-sprint-08-[story-slug].md` and reviewed by qa-lead before story marked COMPLETE. Sessions for LS-007 and LS-008 may be combined into a single playtest pass.

---

## Definition of Done — Sprint 08

A story is DONE when ALL of the following are true:

- [ ] All acceptance criteria verified — via automated test result OR documented manual evidence (screenshot, video, or playtest notes with sign-off)
- [ ] Test file exists at the specified path for all Logic and Integration stories
- [ ] Manual evidence document exists for all Visual/Feel and UI stories (none in this sprint — Config/Data + Logic + Integration only)
- [ ] Smoke check passes (run `/smoke-check sprint` before QA hand-off)
- [ ] No regressions introduced — Sprint 04..07 cumulative suite stays green
- [ ] Code reviewed (via `/code-review` or documented peer review)
- [ ] Story file updated to `Status: Complete` (via `/story-done`)
- [ ] **Sprint-level**: `/scope-check sprint-08-level-streaming-integration` returns 0 additions beyond planned story IDs (LS-004..010 + PIC-FIX)
- [ ] **Sprint-level**: tech-debt register at ≤10 items at sprint close (TD-009 closed by PIC-FIX; baseline 11 → 10)
- [ ] **Sprint-level**: roadmap-close artefact authored — `/asset-spec` hero asset list (Eve FPS hands, Eve full body, PHANTOM grunt + variants, Eiffel bay modules ×3, bomb device, Plaza props)
- [ ] **Sprint-level**: `production/stage.txt` updated from "pre-production" to "art-integration-ready" (surface for user approval)

buffer may populate them if MVP build becomes producible:
